concepts.title: Introduction in Report Writing
concepts.description: A report writing begins with an introduction that provides necessary background information, clearly states the objective of the report, and defines the scope and limitations.

concepts.title: Structure of Report Writing
concepts.description: A report writing consists of an introduction, body, and conclusion. The body is further divided into two main sections: descriptive and analytical.

concepts.title: Importance of Use of Formal Language
concepts.description: Formal language is essential in report writing. It helps to convey information in a clear, concise, and objective manner.

concepts.title: Mnemonic for Introduction, Body, and Conclusion
concepts.description: Use the acronym 'I BC' to remember the structure of a report: Introduction, Body, and Conclusion.

concepts.title: Avoid Using First Person Pronouns
concepts.description: In report writing, avoid using first person pronouns like 'I' or 'me'. This helps to maintain objectivity and neutrality.

concepts.title: Key Features of a Good Report
concepts.description: A good report should have the following key features: clarity, concision, accuracy, objectivity, and relevance.

concepts.title: Importance of Use of Technical Vocabulary
concepts.description: Report writing requires the use of technical vocabulary relevant to the subject. This helps to convey complex ideas and information in a precise manner.

concepts.title: Conclusion in Report Writing
concepts.description: A conclusion in report writing summarizes the main points, reiterates the objective, and provides recommendations or suggestions for future action.
